assess-337a6042.title: Pakistani Police Laws and Regulations — Knowledge Assessment
assess-337a6042.description: AI-generated from actual Pakistani Police Law text.

questions.text: What does the expression 'member of a police-force' mean in the Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922?
options: Any person appointed or enrolled for the performance of police duties under any enactment specified in the Schedule
Any person appointed or enrolled for the performance of police duties under any enactment
Any person who has completed police training
Any person who is a citizen of Pakistan
questions.explanation: The expression 'member of a police-force' is defined in the Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922 as any person appointed or enrolled for the performance of police duties under any enactment specified in the Schedule
questions.law_reference: Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922 — Definition

questions.text: What is required for a Court to proceed to the trial of any offence under the Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922?
options: The previous sanction, or on the complaint, of the District Magistrate
The permission of the Federal Government
The approval of the Provincial Government
The consent of the police force
questions.explanation: No Court shall proceed to the trial of any offence under this Act except with the previous sanction, or on the complaint, of the District Magistrate
questions.law_reference: Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922 — Sanction to trial of offences by subordinate Courts

questions.text: Who is the head of the Airports Security Force?
options: The Director General appointed by the Federal Government
The Inspector General appointed by the Provincial Government
The Chief Executive appointed by the Federal Government
The Commissioner appointed by the Provincial Government
questions.explanation: The head of the Force shall be a Director General appointed by the Federal Government
questions.law_reference: Airports Security Force Act 1975 — Constitution of the Airports Security Force

questions.text: To which areas does the Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922 extend?
options: The whole of Pakistan
Only the Provincial areas
Only the Federal areas
Only the rural areas
questions.explanation: It extends to the whole of Pakistan
questions.law_reference: Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922 — Short title, extent and commencement

questions.text: What is the function of the Airports Security Force?
options: Ensuring security of all airports, aerodromes, aircraft and civil aviation installations
Maintaining law and order in the Provincial areas
Protecting the Federal Government properties
Regulating the movement of vehicles on highways
questions.explanation: The Federal Government may constitute and maintain a force to be called the Airport Security Force for performing the following functions: ensuring security of all airports, aerodromes, aircraft and civil aviation installations
questions.law_reference: Airports Security Force Act 1975 — Power to constitute and maintain the Airports Security Force and its functions

questions.text: Who administers the Airports Security Force?
options: The Director General
The Inspector General
The Chief Executive
The Commissioner
questions.explanation: The administration of the Force shall vest in the Director General who shall administer it in accordance with the Act, rules, and orders of the Federal Government
questions.law_reference: Airports Security Force Act 1975 — Administration of the Force

questions.text: What is the minimum rank of the Court that can try any offence under the Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922?
options: A Magistrate of the first class
A Magistrate of the second class
A Sessions Judge
A High Court Judge
questions.explanation: No Court inferior to that of a Magistrate of the first class shall try any offence under this Act
questions.law_reference: Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922 — Trial of cases

questions.text: What is the function of the Pakistan Railways Police?
options: Preventing loss to Railway property, ensuring proper conduct of persons on Railways, maintaining law and order
Maintaining law and order in the Provincial areas
Protecting the Federal Government properties
Regulating the movement of vehicles on highways
questions.explanation: The Federal Government may constitute, control and maintain a force to be called the Pakistan Railways Police for performing functions within specified territorial limits: preventing loss to Railway property, ensuring proper conduct of persons on Railways, maintaining law and order
questions.law_reference: Pakistan Railways Police Act 1977 — Power to constitute, control and maintain the Pakistan Railways Police and its functions

questions.text: Can a member of the police force of any Province discharge the functions of a police officer in any part of Pakistan beyond his Province?
options: Yes, and shall be deemed to be a member of the police force of that part
No, and shall not be deemed to be a member of the police force of that part
Yes, but only with the permission of the Federal Government
No, but only with the permission of the Provincial Government
questions.explanation: A member of the police force of any Province may discharge the functions of a police officer in any part of Pakistan beyond his Province and shall, while doing so, be deemed to be a member of the police force of that part
questions.law_reference: Police Act 1888 — Employment of police officers beyond the province to which they belong

questions.text: What is the punishment for intentionally causing disaffection towards Pakistan or the Government established by law in Pakistan amongst the members of a police-force?
options: Imprisonment which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to two thousand rupees
Imprisonment which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees
Imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees
Imprisonment which may extend to five years, or with fine which may extend to twenty thousand rupees
questions.explanation: Whoever intentionally causes or attempts to cause, or does any act which he knows is likely to cause, disaffection towards Pakistan or the Government established by law in Pakistan amongst the members of a police-force shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to two thousand rupees
questions.law_reference: Police (Incitement to Disaffection) Act 1922 — Penalty for causing disaffection, etc.

assess-41452a31.title: Pakistani Madrasah Education Board Ordinance 2001: Powers and Functions — Knowledge Assessment
assess-41452a31.description: AI-generated from actual Pakistani Religious Law text.

questions.text: What is required for a court to take cognizance of an offence under the Ehtram-e-Ramazan Ordinance 1981?
options: A complaint in writing by any person
A complaint in writing by a person authorised to arrest
A court can take cognizance without any complaint
A complaint in writing by the Federal Government
questions.explanation: A complaint in writing is required by a person authorised to arrest under sub-section (1) of section 7 or by the officer in charge of the Police-Station.
questions.law_reference: Ehtram-e-Ramazan Ordinance 1981 — Section 7

questions.text: What is the role of the Academic Council of the Pakistan Madrasah Education Board?
options: To advise and assist the Board in its functions
To oversee the financial matters of the Board
To manage the daily operations of the Board
To make laws for the education sector
questions.explanation: The Academic Council advises and assists the Board in the performance of its functions.
questions.law_reference: Pakistan Madrasah Education (Establishment and Affiliation of Model Dini Madaris) Board Ordinance 2001 — Academic Council

questions.text: What is the extent of the Publication of the Holy Quran (Elimination of Printing Errors) Act 1973?
options: Whole of Pakistan
Whole of Islamabad Capital Territory
Whole of Punjab
Whole of Sindh
questions.explanation: The Act extends to the whole of Islamabad Capital Territory.
questions.law_reference: Publication of the Holy Quran (Elimination of Printing Errors) Act 1973 — Section 1

questions.text: What is prohibited during fasting hours in the month of Ramazan under the Ehtram-e-Ramazan Ordinance 1981?
options: Eating, drinking, or smoking in private places
Eating, drinking, or smoking in public places
Not eating, drinking, or smoking at all
Only drinking in public places
questions.explanation: No person who is under an obligation to fast shall eat, drink or smoke in a public place during fasting hours.
questions.law_reference: Ehtram-e-Ramazan Ordinance 1981 — Prohibition of eating, etc., in public places

questions.text: What is the penalty for contravening the provisions of the Publication of the Holy Quran (Elimination of Printing Errors) Act 1973?
options: Imprisonment for a term which may extend to 1 year, or with fine
Imprisonment for a term which may extend to 3 years, or with fine
Imprisonment for a term which may extend to 5 years, or with fine
Imprisonment for a term which may extend to 10 years, or with fine
questions.explanation: The penalty is imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine amounting to not less than twenty thousand rupees.
questions.law_reference: Publication of the Holy Quran (Elimination of Printing Errors) Act 1973 — Penalty

questions.text: What is the composition of the Quran Board under the Publication of the Holy Quran (Elimination of Printing Errors) Act 1973?
options: Prominent Ulema, Huffaz and Qaris of all schools of thought amongst Muslims
Only prominent Ulema amongst Muslims
Only Huffaz amongst Muslims
Only Qaris amongst Muslims
questions.explanation: The Quran Board comprises prominent Ulema, Huffaz and Qaris of all schools of thought amongst Muslims.
questions.law_reference: Publication of the Holy Quran (Elimination of Printing Errors) Act 1973 — Quran Board

questions.text: What is the supreme law of Pakistan under the Enforcement of Shari'ah Act 1991?
options: The Constitution of Pakistan
The Shari'ah
The laws enacted by the Parliament
The laws enacted by the Provincial Assembly
questions.explanation: The Shari'ah, that is to say, the Injunctions of Islam as laid down in the Holy Quran and Sunnah, shall be the supreme law of Pakistan.
questions.law_reference: Enforcement of Shari'ah Act 1991 — Supremacy of Shari'ah

questions.text: Who has the power to enter and arrest under the Ehtram-e-Ramazan Ordinance 1981?
options: Any person
A Magistrate, the Chairman of a District Council or a Municipal Committee
The Federal Government
The Supreme Court
questions.explanation: A Magistrate, the Chairman of a District Council or a Municipal Committee or a Town Committee, the Mayor of a Municipal Corporation, or the Chairman or a Member of a District Zakat and Ushr Committee has the power to enter and arrest.
questions.law_reference: Ehtram-e-Ramazan Ordinance 1981 — Power to enter and arrest

questions.text: What is the role of the Chairman of the Pakistan Madrasah Education Board?
options: To oversee the financial matters of the Board
To manage the daily operations of the Board
To preside over the meetings of the Board and be the principal executive officer
To make laws for the education sector
questions.explanation: The Chairman shall be the principal executive officer and shall preside over the meetings of the Board.
questions.law_reference: Pakistan Madrasah Education (Establishment and Affiliation of Model Dini Madaris) Board Ordinance 2001 — Chairman

questions.text: What is the State's responsibility regarding the rights of women under the Enforcement of Shari'ah Act 1991?
options: To affect the rights of women as guaranteed by the Constitution
To not affect the rights of women as guaranteed by the Constitution
To enact new laws for the rights of women
To eliminate the rights of women
questions.explanation: The rights of women as guaranteed by the Constitution shall not be affected.
questions.law_reference: Enforcement of Shari'ah Act 1991 — Rights of women not to be affected
